setting_name: Set up a child account in Family Sharing
what_it_does: Creates an Apple Account for your child inside your family group with age-based protections applied automatically
why_it_matters: Everything else on Apple devices hangs off this. Age must be correct because iOS uses it to decide which protections apply
how_to_path: Settings > [Your Name] > Family Sharing > Add Member > Create Child Account
bypass_risk: Child entering a false birthday at setup; check the age on the account

what_it_does: A 4-digit code your child does not know that locks all parental settings in place
why_it_matters: Without it every other setting can be switched off in seconds
how_to_path: Settings > Screen Time > Lock Screen Time Settings
bypass_risk: Children guessing birthdays or watching you type it; choose a code they cannot guess and shield entry

what_it_does: The master switch for purchase blocks; app age limits; web filters and privacy locks
why_it_matters: Turning this off pauses every restriction underneath it
how_to_path: Settings > Screen Time > Content & Privacy Restrictions > On
default_state: On by default for child accounts in some regions
bypass_risk: None while passcode holds

setting_name: Web content filter and Ask to Browse
what_it_does: Limits adult websites or restricts Safari to approved sites only. Ask to Browse (iOS 27) makes children request each new website
why_it_matters: Websites are the classic route around app blocks
how_to_path: Settings > Screen Time > Content & Privacy Restrictions > Content Restrictions > Web Content
default_state: Limit Adult Websites on for under 18s
bypass_risk: Other browsers if installed; pair with app approval

what_it_does: Detects nudity in photos and videos before they are sent or viewed and adds a pause with help resources
why_it_matters: First line against sexting pressure and unsolicited images. On automatically for under 18s
how_to_path: Settings > Screen Time > Communication Safety
bypass_risk: None significant

what_it_does: Controls who your child can call and message and requires your approval when they add a new contact
why_it_matters: Directly limits stranger contact through Phone; FaceTime and Messages
how_to_path: Settings > Screen Time > Communication Limits
bypass_risk: Third party apps have their own messaging; cover those per platform

what_it_does: Every app download and in-app purchase needs your approval; free or paid
why_it_matters: Stops surprise spending and blocks unapproved apps in one move
how_to_path: Family Sharing > [Child] > Ask to Buy
bypass_risk: Gift cards preloaded to the account

what_it_does: Blocks apps and notifications during hours you set; only allowed apps and calls work
why_it_matters: Protects sleep and homework. Sleep loss is one of the best evidenced screen harms
how_to_path: Settings > Screen Time > Downtime
bypass_risk: None while passcode holds

what_it_does: Daily time caps on app categories such as games and social; with expert-informed defaults by age in iOS 27
why_it_matters: Caps the endless scroll without banning the app outright
how_to_path: Settings > Screen Time > App Limits
bypass_risk: Time limit nagging; approve extensions deliberately not reflexively

what_it_does: Hides apps above the age rating you choose; using the expanded 13+ 16+ 18+ bands
why_it_matters: Stops age-inappropriate apps at the front door
how_to_path: Settings > Screen Time > Content & Privacy Restrictions > Content Restrictions > Apps
bypass_risk: Web versions of blocked apps; pair with web filter

what_it_does: Shares your child's location with the family and prevents them changing privacy settings
why_it_matters: Useful for safety and stops apps quietly harvesting location
how_to_path: Settings > Screen Time > Content & Privacy Restrictions > Privacy
bypass_risk: Device off or left behind

what_it_does: Links your child's Google account to yours for remote control of apps; time; content and location
why_it_matters: The foundation for everything on Android. Account level so filters survive switching browsers
how_to_path: Family Link app > Add child > follow prompts on both devices
bypass_risk: At 13 the child can end supervision; you are notified. Have the conversation before the birthday

what_it_does: Requires your approval for Google Play downloads and purchases
why_it_matters: Blocks unapproved apps and surprise spending together
how_to_path: Family Link > Controls > Content restrictions > Google Play
default_state: Approval for paid; check free apps too
bypass_risk: APK sideloading on some devices; block unknown sources in device settings

what_it_does: Caps apps games films and books by age rating
why_it_matters: Front door filter for age-inappropriate content
how_to_path: Family Link > Controls > Content restrictions > Google Play > ratings
bypass_risk: Web content; pair with Chrome filter

what_it_does: Filters explicit results from Google Search and blocks adult or listed sites in Chrome
why_it_matters: Account level so it holds across the device not just one browser
how_to_path: Family Link > Controls > Content restrictions > Google Search / Google Chrome
bypass_risk: Other search engines inside other apps

what_it_does: Routes under 13s to YouTube Kids or a supervised experience with a content level you choose
why_it_matters: YouTube is where most child screen time actually lives
how_to_path: Family Link > Controls > Content restrictions > YouTube
bypass_risk: Signed-out browsing on other devices

setting_name: Daily limit; Downtime and School Time
what_it_does: Daily device cap plus scheduled blocks for bedtime and school hours
why_it_matters: Structure beats willpower; theirs and yours
how_to_path: Family Link > Screen time > Daily limit / Schedules
bypass_risk: Limits are per device; set on every device the child uses

what_it_does: Block individual apps or cap them while leaving useful apps unlimited
why_it_matters: Precision beats blanket bans; keeps homework tools open while capping games
how_to_path: Family Link > Screen time > App limits
bypass_risk: New lookalike apps; review the app list monthly

what_it_does: See your child's device location on a map with arrival and departure alerts
why_it_matters: Independence with a safety net
how_to_path: Family Link > Location
bypass_risk: Device off

setting_name: Microsoft family group and child account
what_it_does: Adds your child to a Microsoft family group covering Windows and Xbox together
why_it_matters: One setup covers PC and console
how_to_path: account.microsoft.com/family > Add a family member
bypass_risk: Local accounts outside the family group; make the child account the only sign-in

what_it_does: Blocks adult sites and forces SafeSearch in Edge; can block other browsers
why_it_matters: Windows filtering only holds inside Edge so lock the browser choice too
how_to_path: Family Safety app > [Child] > Content filters > Web and search
bypass_risk: Other browsers; enable Only use allowed websites or block browser installs

what_it_does: Caps apps and games by age rating; child can request exceptions
why_it_matters: PC gaming is the least controlled gaming surface; this is the baseline
how_to_path: Family Safety app > [Child] > Content filters > Apps and games
bypass_risk: Steam and Epic have separate stores; set their family controls separately

what_it_does: Daily allowances and permitted hours per device or shared across devices
why_it_matters: Same structure principle as phones; sleep and homework protected
how_to_path: Family Safety app > [Child] > Screen time
bypass_risk: None significant while account holds

what_it_does: A walled-garden child profile with only approved apps books and videos
why_it_matters: The strongest default environment of any mainstream tablet
how_to_path: Settings > Profiles & Family Library > Add child profile
bypass_risk: Exiting to the adult profile; PIN-protect the exit and your profile

what_it_does: Daily caps by activity type and a Learn First mode that unlocks entertainment after reading
why_it_matters: Rewards structure built in; use it
how_to_path: Parent Dashboard > [Child] > Set daily goals & time limits
bypass_risk: None significant

what_it_does: Disables the browser in the child profile or limits it to curated sites
why_it_matters: The browser is the hole in every walled garden
how_to_path: Parent Dashboard > [Child] > Web settings
bypass_risk: None significant inside the profile

what_it_does: Blocks purchases inside the child profile entirely
why_it_matters: Children cannot consent to spending; remove the surface
how_to_path: Settings > Applications > Amazon Application Settings > Appstore > In-App Purchasing
bypass_risk: Adult profile access

setting_name: Link the Parental Controls app and set a PIN
what_it_does: Pairs the console to your phone; a PIN then gates every restriction change
why_it_matters: Console-side controls are limited; the app is where the real controls live
how_to_path: Download Nintendo Switch Parental Controls app > link console > set PIN
bypass_risk: PIN observed over your shoulder; change it if guessed

what_it_does: Preset Child; Pre-Teen or Teen levels controlling game ratings and features; or custom
why_it_matters: One choice sets game age ratings across the console. Set for the youngest player; controls are per console not per child
how_to_path: App > Console Settings > Restriction Level
bypass_risk: Whitelisted games bypass ratings; review the safelist

what_it_does: Daily play cap and a bedtime cut-off with optional automatic game suspension
why_it_matters: Gaming session creep is the number one console flashpoint; automate the boundary
how_to_path: App > Play Time Limit / Bedtime
bypass_risk: Alarm-only mode just notifies; enable Suspend Software to enforce

what_it_does: Blocks in-game chat; messaging and sharing of user content; per game or globally
why_it_matters: Stranger contact in games is the risk parents most underestimate
how_to_path: App > Console Settings > Communicating with Others
default_state: Restricted at Child and Pre-Teen levels
bypass_risk: Voice chat through phone apps like Discord sits outside the console

what_it_does: Blocks or password-gates purchases on Nintendo eShop via your Nintendo Account
why_it_matters: Spending controls live in the Nintendo Account not the console app; families miss this
how_to_path: accounts.nintendo.com > Family group > [Child] > restrict spending
bypass_risk: Gift cards redeem regardless; keep balances low

what_it_does: Voice and video chat on Switch 2; under 16s need parent setup and can only chat with friends you approve; chat history reviewable
why_it_matters: New surface on Switch 2; approval-based friends list is the control that matters
how_to_path: Parental Controls app > GameChat settings
bypass_risk: None significant; approval model is strong

what_it_does: Creates a child account in your PlayStation family with age-appropriate presets
why_it_matters: Presets apply sensible defaults per age in one tap; customise from there
how_to_path: PlayStation Family app > Add child
bypass_risk: Child using a parent's signed-in profile; passcode-lock your own profile

what_it_does: Blocks games above the PEGI rating for the child's age; child can request exceptions you approve
why_it_matters: Ratings enforcement with a built-in negotiation channel; better than silent blocks
how_to_path: Family app > [Child] > Content restrictions
bypass_risk: Disc games from friends still boot on adult profiles

what_it_does: Daily playtime per day of week with a 15 minute warning; child can request more from the console
why_it_matters: Session structure with flexibility built in
how_to_path: Family app > [Child] > Playtime settings
bypass_risk: None significant

what_it_does: Caps PlayStation Store spending per month; you add funds and see balances
why_it_matters: In-game purchase pressure is constant; a hard monthly cap ends the argument
how_to_path: Family app > [Child] > Spending limit
bypass_risk: Gift cards; keep them occasional

what_it_does: Blocks text; voice and video messages and viewing or sharing user-generated content; per-game exceptions on request
why_it_matters: Cuts stranger contact while letting you approve trusted games like Minecraft
how_to_path: Family app > [Child] > Communication and UGC
bypass_risk: Party chat via phone apps sits outside the console

setting_name: Child account in Xbox Family Settings app
what_it_does: Adds your child to your Microsoft family and manages everything from your phone in real time
why_it_matters: The best console management app; also covers Windows PCs
how_to_path: Xbox Family Settings app > Add child
bypass_risk: Child signing in as another user; PIN your own profile

what_it_does: Filters games apps and media to the age you confirm; per-title exceptions approvable
why_it_matters: One age setting maps to PEGI ratings across everything
how_to_path: Family app > [Child] > Content restrictions > Allow content suitable for age
bypass_risk: Physical discs on unrestricted profiles

what_it_does: Every purchase request comes to your phone or email for approval
why_it_matters: Kills accidental and pester-power spending
how_to_path: Family app > [Child] > Content restrictions > Needs adult approval to buy
bypass_risk: Gift card balances spend freely; monitor balance in app

what_it_does: Daily allowances and permitted hours; pause play instantly from your phone
why_it_matters: The remote pause is the single most useful console feature for dinner time
how_to_path: Family app > [Child] > Screen time
bypass_risk: None significant

what_it_does: Controls voice and text chat; who can communicate; multiplayer; clubs and cross-network play
why_it_matters: Cross-network play connects your child to PC and PlayStation strangers; decide deliberately
how_to_path: Family app > [Child] > Privacy & online safety
bypass_risk: Discord on phone bypasses console chat controls

what_it_does: Links the child's account for app approval; time insights and feature control. Auto-enrolled ages 10 to 12
why_it_matters: VR is more immersive than any flat screen; supervision is non-negotiable at this age
how_to_path: Meta Horizon app > Family Center > link accounts
bypass_risk: Using a parent or sibling account; keep yours locked

what_it_does: Approve each app your child downloads and cap by age rating
why_it_matters: VR content ratings run ahead of parental awareness; approve per title
how_to_path: Family Center > [Child] > App approvals
bypass_risk: None significant while supervision holds

setting_name: Cast the headset to a phone or TV
what_it_does: Mirrors what your child sees in the headset to a screen you can see
why_it_matters: The only way to actually see inside VR; make casting the house norm
how_to_path: Quest headset > Camera > Cast
bypass_risk: None; it is a norm not a lock

setting_name: Kids profiles in every streaming app
what_it_does: Child profiles with maturity limits in Netflix Disney+ and others on the TV
why_it_matters: The living room TV is the most shared and least configured screen in the house
how_to_path: Per app: Profiles > Add kids profile
bypass_risk: Switching to an adult profile; PIN every adult profile

setting_name: TV app store and purchase PIN
what_it_does: PIN-gates app installs and purchases on the TV OS itself
why_it_matters: Stops new unvetted apps arriving on the family screen
how_to_path: TV Settings > Parental controls / Store settings
bypass_risk: None significant

setting_name: YouTube on TV signed into the supervised account
what_it_does: Applies your child's YouTube content level and autoplay settings on the big screen
why_it_matters: Signed-out TV YouTube has no filters at all; this closes the biggest living room gap
how_to_path: YouTube app on TV > sign in with child's supervised account
bypass_risk: Signing out; check the account chip periodically

what_it_does: Network-level blocking of adult and harmful categories on every device using home wifi
why_it_matters: Covers guest devices; old tablets and anything you forgot. BT Sky Virgin and TalkTalk all include it free
how_to_path: ISP account dashboard > Parental controls / Web safe
bypass_risk: Mobile data and VPNs bypass it; treat it as one layer of several

what_it_does: Pauses internet to chosen devices at bedtime or homework hours from the router app
why_it_matters: Enforces the household rhythm at the pipe; no per-device negotiation
how_to_path: ISP or router app > Device schedules / Pause
bypass_risk: Mobile data on phones

what_it_does: Kid-safe mode with explicit lyrics filtered; purchasing off and kid skills only
why_it_matters: Smart speakers answer any question a child asks; scope what they can reach
how_to_path: Alexa app > Settings > Amazon Kids
bypass_risk: Other speakers in the house without kids mode

what_it_does: Limits who the device can call and who can drop in unannounced
why_it_matters: Drop-in is effectively an open intercom; restrict it to household
how_to_path: Alexa app > Settings > Communication
bypass_risk: None significant

setting_name: Confirm Teen Account is active with real birthday
what_it_does: Auto protections for under 18s: private account; DM limits; sleep mode 10pm to 7am; 13+ content tier; 60 minute reminder
why_it_matters: The protections only exist if the account age is real. Accounts created with a false adult birthday get none of it
how_to_path: Settings > Account type and tools; correct birthday if wrong
bypass_risk: False birthday at signup; Meta age-detection AI catches some but not all. Ask to see the account type together

setting_name: Set up Supervision in Family Center
what_it_does: Links your account to your teen's: see time spent; followers; DM settings; set daily limits; approve setting changes; get suicide and self-harm search alerts
why_it_matters: Under 16s need your approval to loosen any protection only if supervision is on. One invite now covers Instagram Facebook and Messenger
how_to_path: familycenter.meta.com/supervision > send invite
bypass_risk: Teen can end supervision; you are notified immediately

what_it_does: Only approved followers see posts and stories
why_it_matters: Default for teens; under 16s need your permission to go public. Public teen accounts attract adult strangers
how_to_path: Settings > Account privacy
bypass_risk: Second public account (finsta); supervision does not see other accounts

what_it_does: Only people they follow or existing connections can DM or add them to groups
why_it_matters: Stranger DMs are the front door for grooming and sextortion
how_to_path: Settings > Messages and story replies
bypass_risk: Following back a stranger opens the channel; talk about follow-backs

what_it_does: Filters offensive words and phrases from comments and DM requests
why_it_matters: The strongest anti-bullying filter on the platform; on max for teen accounts
how_to_path: Settings > Hidden Words
bypass_risk: New slang outpaces filters

what_it_does: Mutes notifications 10pm to 7am and auto-replies to DMs
why_it_matters: Night notifications are the sleep killer; this removes the pull
how_to_path: On automatically for teen accounts; verify in Settings
bypass_risk: Opening the app manually still works; pair with device Downtime

what_it_does: Set a hard daily cap on Instagram from your supervision dashboard
why_it_matters: Turns the 60 minute nudge into an actual boundary
how_to_path: Family Center > [Teen] > Time limits
bypass_risk: Web browser access; cover with device-level limits

what_it_does: Optional tier above the default 13+ content standard filtering more suggestive and borderline content
why_it_matters: For younger teens or after a bad content experience; you approve any change back
how_to_path: Family Center > [Teen] > Content preferences
bypass_risk: Content in DMs is not filtered by tier

what_it_does: Links your TikTok account to your teen's for remote control of time; content and privacy settings
why_it_matters: The teen can tighten settings themselves but cannot loosen what you set
how_to_path: Profile > Menu > Settings and privacy > Family Pairing > scan QR codes
bypass_risk: Teen can unlink; you are notified. A second account is invisible to pairing

what_it_does: Caps TikTok per day across all your child's devices; passcode needed to continue
why_it_matters: Default is 60 minutes for 13 to 17s. Prevents logging out to dodge the limit
how_to_path: Family Pairing > Screen time; or Settings > Screen time
bypass_risk: Watching TikToks reposted to YouTube Shorts and Instagram Reels

what_it_does: Filters mature and complex themes from the feed; locked from your account so they cannot toggle it off
why_it_matters: Imperfect but meaningful reduction in mature content exposure
how_to_path: Family Pairing > Restricted Mode
bypass_risk: Filter misses borderline content; report what slips through

what_it_does: Only approved followers see videos; account not suggested to others
why_it_matters: Default private for under 16s; verify it has not been switched
how_to_path: Settings > Privacy > Private account / Suggest your account to others
bypass_risk: Second account

what_it_does: Restrict who can DM your teen or turn DMs off; DMs are 16+ on TikTok anyway
why_it_matters: Confirm the age on the account is real so the 16+ DM wall actually applies
how_to_path: Family Pairing > Direct messages
bypass_risk: False birthday unlocks DMs

what_it_does: Turn search off or limit what content users hashtags and sounds they can search
why_it_matters: Search is how children pull risky content instead of waiting for the algorithm to push it
how_to_path: Family Pairing > Search
bypass_risk: Trending content still lands in the feed

what_it_does: See your teen's friends list and who they messaged in the last 7 days; not the content
why_it_matters: Visibility into who not what; deliberately privacy-preserving. Pair with conversation
how_to_path: Settings > Privacy Controls > Family Centre > invite
bypass_risk: Teen can leave Family Centre

what_it_does: Hides your child's live location from everyone on the map
why_it_matters: Live location broadcast to a friends list is a physical safety issue not a digital one
how_to_path: Settings > See My Location > Ghost Mode
default_state: Location visible to friends once map opened
bypass_risk: None while set

what_it_does: Only added friends can snap or chat your child
why_it_matters: The single most important Snapchat setting against stranger contact
how_to_path: Settings > Contact Me > My Friends
bypass_risk: Accepting stranger friend requests; talk about who counts as a friend

what_it_does: Stops your child being suggested as a friend to strangers and subscription channels
why_it_matters: Quick Add is how strangers find children at scale
how_to_path: Settings > See Me in Quick Add > off
bypass_risk: None while set

what_it_does: Only added friends can view your child's story
why_it_matters: Public stories advertise a child's life to strangers
how_to_path: Settings > View My Story > My Friends
bypass_risk: Custom lists that include loosely known contacts

what_it_does: Removes or limits the built-in AI chatbot from your child's chats
why_it_matters: An always-on AI companion in a teen's DMs deserves a deliberate decision not a default
how_to_path: Family Centre > Settings > restrict My AI
bypass_risk: None while restricted

what_it_does: Restricts suggestive and sensitive content in Stories and Spotlight
why_it_matters: Spotlight is Snapchat's TikTok; filter it the same way
how_to_path: Family Centre > Settings > Restrict sensitive content
bypass_risk: DM content unfiltered

setting_name: Choose the right YouTube tier for the age
what_it_does: YouTube Kids app for 4 to 8; supervised kid account with content level for 8 to 12; teen account with supervision 13+
why_it_matters: The tier decision matters more than any single toggle inside it
how_to_path: families.youtube.com > set up per child
bypass_risk: Signed-out YouTube in a browser has no protections at all

what_it_does: Restricts YouTube Kids to videos and channels you have personally approved; search off
why_it_matters: The strongest setting on any video platform for young children
how_to_path: YouTube Kids > Settings > [Child] > Approved content only
bypass_risk: Handing over an adult device with normal YouTube

what_it_does: Explore (9+); Explore More (13+) or Most of YouTube; applies on every signed-in device
why_it_matters: One setting follows the account everywhere including the TV
how_to_path: Family Link > Controls > Content restrictions > YouTube
bypass_risk: Signing out; restrict account changes via device settings

what_it_does: Stops the next video starting automatically; child cannot re-enable on supervised accounts
why_it_matters: Autoplay is the engine of accidental hours and content drift
how_to_path: Family Center > [Child] > Disable autoplay
bypass_risk: None while supervised

what_it_does: Caps daily Shorts scrolling on supervised teen accounts; zero option from March 2026
why_it_matters: Industry-first control on short-form feeds; the highest-compulsion format
how_to_path: Family Center > [Teen] > Time management > Shorts limit
bypass_risk: Reels and TikTok deliver the same format; set limits there too

setting_name: Bedtime and take a break reminders
what_it_does: Custom reminders on supervised teen accounts building on default-on wellbeing protections
why_it_matters: Reinforces the same sleep boundary set at device level
how_to_path: Family Center > [Teen] > Time management
bypass_risk: Reminders are dismissible; they are nudges not locks

what_it_does: You set up and control the account: approve contacts and groups; review unknown message requests; settings gated by parent PIN
why_it_matters: Purpose-built pre-teen messaging with parent approval on every new contact; messaging is excluded from the UK ban so this is the pathway app
how_to_path: Set up with both phones side by side per WhatsApp flow
bypass_risk: SIM swap to a fresh number restarts unmanaged; keep the number in your name

what_it_does: Only saved contacts can add your child to group chats
why_it_matters: Random group adds are the WhatsApp bullying and scam vector
how_to_path: Settings > Privacy > Groups > My Contacts
bypass_risk: Being added by a contact to a group full of strangers; teach the exit

setting_name: Profile photo; last seen and about to contacts only
what_it_does: Strangers see nothing about your child
why_it_matters: Profile photos of children should not be publicly scrapeable
how_to_path: Settings > Privacy > set each to My Contacts
bypass_risk: None while set

what_it_does: Only share live location deliberately and briefly with family
why_it_matters: Persistent location shares to group chats leak physical patterns
how_to_path: Check active shares: Settings > Privacy > Live location
bypass_risk: Re-sharing in the moment; make it a named family rule

what_it_does: A PIN that stops anyone hijacking the WhatsApp account with a stolen code
why_it_matters: Account takeover scams target teens; this closes the door
how_to_path: Settings > Account > Two-step verification
bypass_risk: Phishing for the PIN itself; never share it with anyone

what_it_does: Connects your age-checked account to your child's for full parental controls to age 15
why_it_matters: Everything on Roblox now routes through this link including chat permissions and Trusted Friends approvals
how_to_path: Roblox Settings > Parental Controls > link account
bypass_risk: Child on an unlinked account with a false age; June 2026 age checks close much of this

setting_name: Correct account type: Kids or Select
what_it_does: Roblox Kids (5 to 8) has strictest defaults with chat off; Select (9 to 15) unlocks gradually; 16+ standard after age check
why_it_matters: Worldwide from June 2026: no age check means no chat at all. The account type is the biggest single protection
how_to_path: Automatic via age check; verify in account settings
bypass_risk: Transition period allows self-declared age for game access

what_it_does: Caps games by maturity rating; moving to PEGI ratings; block or allow individual games
why_it_matters: User-generated platform so ratings vary; per-game block list is your scalpel
how_to_path: Settings > Parental Controls > Content maturity / Allowed experiences
default_state: Minimal to Moderate by account type
bypass_risk: Experiences misrated by creators; report and block

what_it_does: Separate controls for in-game chat; direct messages and party chat; off by default under 9
why_it_matters: Chat is where grooming happens; know the three channels and set each
how_to_path: Parental Controls > Communication
bypass_risk: Moving the chat to Discord or Snapchat; the migration is the risk

what_it_does: Cross-age chat only with Trusted Friends; under 13 additions need your approval; review the list monthly
why_it_matters: Parents are not proactively notified of every teen connection; the review habit is on you
how_to_path: Parental Controls > Connections / Trusted Friends
bypass_risk: In-person QR verification abused by older contacts met offline

what_it_does: Caps Robux purchases per month with visibility of spend
why_it_matters: Robux pressure is constant and social; a named monthly number ends negotiation
how_to_path: Parental Controls > Spending
bypass_risk: Gift cards from relatives; brief the grandparents

what_it_does: Daily Roblox time cap set from the parent dashboard
why_it_matters: In-platform cap that survives device switching
how_to_path: Parental Controls > Screen time
bypass_risk: Browser Roblox on another device; pair with device limits

what_it_does: Under 13s get a limited account until a parent consents; PIN gates all settings
why_it_matters: Epic's consent gate is real; do not enter your PIN to wave everything through
how_to_path: Epic account > Parental controls > set PIN
bypass_risk: Borrowed older accounts; check whose account is signed in

what_it_does: Turn voice chat off or friends-only; filter mature language in text
why_it_matters: Squad voice chat with strangers is default culture in Fortnite; decide deliberately
how_to_path: Parental controls > Voice / Text chat
bypass_risk: Discord voice on the side

what_it_does: Requires PIN or approval for V-Bucks and item shop purchases
why_it_matters: The item shop is engineered FOMO; the approval step interrupts it
how_to_path: Parental controls > Purchasing
bypass_risk: Gift cards

what_it_does: Multiplayer; clubs and chat in Minecraft follow the child's Xbox account settings on every platform including phones
why_it_matters: Families miss that a Microsoft setting controls Minecraft on an iPad
how_to_path: account.xbox.com/settings > [Child] > Xbox privacy
bypass_risk: Third-party servers have their own chat cultures; know which servers

what_it_does: Private invite-only worlds instead of public servers
why_it_matters: Public servers are unmoderated; Realms keeps multiplayer inside the friend group
how_to_path: Play on a family-owned Realm; disable server joins in Xbox privacy
bypass_risk: Friend codes shared onward

what_it_does: Weekly summary of servers joined and users messaged; no message content
why_it_matters: Discord is where locked-down chats migrate to; visibility here catches the migration
how_to_path: User Settings > Family Center > invite
bypass_risk: Teen can disable; alt accounts invisible

what_it_does: Scans and blocks explicit images in DMs; restricts DMs from non-friends
why_it_matters: Direct image filtering matters on a platform with no content moderation baseline in private spaces
how_to_path: User Settings > Privacy & Safety > Keep me safe
bypass_risk: Links out to unfiltered content

what_it_does: Anyone can create a server; know which communities your teen is in
why_it_matters: Server culture varies from homework groups to the genuinely dark; the review is a conversation not a search
how_to_path: Family Center weekly summary + monthly chat
bypass_risk: Second account

setting_name: Teen Account active on Facebook and Messenger
what_it_does: 13+ content settings and contact limits extended to Facebook and Messenger globally from June 2026
why_it_matters: Younger teens live in Messenger group chats even when they ignore the Facebook feed
how_to_path: Automatic under 18 with real birthday; verify account type
bypass_risk: False birthday

what_it_does: Per-profile content caps with a simplified kids interface
why_it_matters: Ninety seconds of setup for the most watched app in most homes
how_to_path: Profiles > Add profile > Kids; set maturity rating
bypass_risk: Switching to an unlocked adult profile

what_it_does: Requires a PIN to open any profile above the child's rating
why_it_matters: Closes the profile-switch loophole
how_to_path: Account > Profile & parental controls > Profile lock
bypass_risk: Watching over a sibling's shoulder

what_it_does: Kid profile with simplified UI and content caps; kids profile exit protection
why_it_matters: Same principle as Netflix; do both at once
how_to_path: Profiles > Edit > Junior Mode / content rating + PIN adult profiles
bypass_risk: Profile switching without exit protection

what_it_does: Blocks tracks tagged explicit on the child's account
why_it_matters: Partial because podcasts are largely untagged; still worth setting
how_to_path: Settings > Content preferences > Allow explicit content off
bypass_risk: Podcasts and user uploads slip the tags

setting_name: Honest position: no parental controls exist
what_it_does: X has no supervision or family tools and permits adult content on platform
why_it_matters: Settings cannot make X age appropriate for under 16s. The honest guidance is the pathway not the toggle. Named in scope for the UK ban
how_to_path: No parent tooling; account-level sensitive media settings only
bypass_risk: None; the gap is structural
